report_text: FINDINGS: No focal consolidation. No pleural effusion. Cardiomediastinal silhouette is within normal limits.
FINDINGS: Mild left pleural effusion. No pneumothorax.
FINDINGS: Lungs are clear. No acute cardiopulmonary process.
FINDINGS: Mild bibasilar atelectatic change. No focal airspace opacity.
FINDINGS: Small bilateral pleural effusions with bibasilar atelectasis. Cardiac silhouette is mildly enlarged.
FINDINGS: Patchy right lower lobe opacity may represent developing infiltrate. Trace left pleural effusion.
FINDINGS: Mild pulmonary vascular congestion and cardiomegaly. No pneumothorax.
FINDINGS: Left basilar opacity likely atelectatic, cannot exclude early pneumonia.
FINDINGS: Interval increase in bibasilar interstitial opacities. Small right pleural effusion.
FINDINGS: Low lung volume exam with bibasilar crowding. No focal consolidation.
FINDINGS: Endotracheal tube tip projects 3 cm above carina. Enteric tube courses below diaphragm. Bilateral perihilar opacities and small pleural effusions suggest pulmonary edema.
FINDINGS: Poststernotomy changes. Persistent left retrocardiac opacity with volume loss. Small left pleural effusion. Mild right basilar streaky opacity.
FINDINGS: Multifocal bilateral patchy opacities, right greater than left, with probable superimposed bibasilar atelectasis. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.
FINDINGS: Cardiomediastinal silhouette remains enlarged. Diffuse interstitial opacities with bilateral small pleural effusions, favored CHF pattern.
FINDINGS: Tiny right apical pneumothorax is present. Recommend close follow-up radiograph.
FINDINGS: No pleural effusion, no focal consolidation, no pulmonary edema, no pneumothorax.
FINDINGS: Possible left lower lobe infiltrate. Clinical correlation is advised.
FINDINGS: Cannot exclude subtle left basilar airspace disease on this single portable view.
FINDINGS: New right upper lobe nodule compared with prior exam. No pleural effusion.
FINDINGS: Bibasilar opacities may represent atelectasis though infection is not excluded.
